role_title: Head of Client Strategy and Operations

seniority.level: Director / VP (senior IC or player-coach, not large org owner)
level_signals: 12+ years in strategy, operations, transformation, or client-facing leadership roles
Role explicitly says: 'You will not own the global services organization or manage large execution teams directly'
Profile signals include Chief of Staff to CRO, COO, GM, or CEO

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 12+ years in strategy, operations, transformation, or client-facing leadership roles within complex B2B SaaS, fintech, or data platforms
Experience operating inside organizations with long, multi-stakeholder client implementations
Proven exposure to companies scaling up from 1,000+ employees (or similar complexity)
Hands-on leader experienced in deploying complex operating models and technologies where success is defined by seamless execution and organizational buy-in, not just design
Comfortable operating in ambiguity and change, helping organizations evolve while continuing to deliver while scaling structure in fast-moving environments
Able to balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution
Strong cross-functional credibility - senior enough to influence executives, pragmatic enough to build and iterate
Strong analytical skills; ability to build and maintain data models, define metrics, and translate data into executive-ready narratives
Strong communicator — equally effective in a strategic planning session and a process documentation review

Applicants must be legally authorized to work in the United States for any employer without requiring current or future visa sponsorship (for example, employment-based visas such as H-1B, F-1/OPT, or similar), and must be authorized to begin work in the U.S. on their first day of employment.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Chief of Staff to CRO, COO, GM, or CEO
Strategy & Operations leader embedded in a commercial or client organization
Operating model or transformation leader inside a product-driven company

explicitly_excluded: Candidates requiring current or future visa sponsorship (H-1B, F-1/OPT, or similar)

scope.surface: Client Strategy & Operations across onboarding, implementation, service delivery, renewals, partner programs, and AI/automation adoption
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / data platform (wealth management / investment management)
core_responsibilities: Translate client strategy into operating priorities, sequencing, and decision frameworks
Own and maintain the operating calendar, decision logs, and priority sequencing artifacts
Define and monitor client-facing and operational metrics; own the client operations data model
Define and evolve the client operating model including handoffs, accountability, and cross-functional dependencies
Support transition to AI-enabled client model; ensure changes are intentional, sequenced, and adopted
Lead operational strategy and planning for Addepar's partner programs
Partner with Client Leadership to define strategic role of AI and automation
Serve as senior integrator across Client Services, Product, Engineering, Data, RevOps, and Finance
Partner with Sales and RevOps on GTM planning, capacity inputs, and operational readiness

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($171K-$268K base) plus bonus and equity
Role scope is clearly articulated — explicit about what this role does NOT own, reducing ambiguity
Senior strategic role with influence-based leadership model rather than large headcount management
Addepar is at meaningful scale ($9T AUM, 1,400+ firms, nearly 60 countries) — strong platform for impact
Clear articulation of success metrics (clarity, alignment, forward momentum)
red_flags: No visa sponsorship — strict US work authorization requirement eliminates a broad candidate pool
Location type is ambiguous: JD mentions Colorado, California, and New York for comp ranges but does not explicitly state if role is remote, hybrid, or onsite
Broad comp range ($171K-$268K, ~57% spread) suggests high variance depending on location or seniority calibration
Role requires influence without authority across a large cross-functional set — success heavily dependent on organizational culture and executive support not guaranteed by job design
Profile signals section lists three archetypes ('any combination') which may indicate role definition is still evolving

fields_inferred_not_stated: location.type (not explicitly stated as remote/hybrid/onsite; inferred as unclear)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred true from multi-state comp ranges and no office requirement stated)
seniority.level (inferred from comp range and 12+ years requirement; Director/VP level)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description — B2B SaaS/data platform for wealth management)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not exhaustively stated in JD)

ambiguities: Location type is not stated explicitly. The JD references comp ranges for Colorado, California, and New York but does not specify if the role is remote, hybrid, or onsite. Treated as 'unclear'.
Profile signals section says 'any combination' of three archetypes — unclear if these are must-haves or nice-to-haves. Treated as nice-to-haves given the phrasing 'Profile Signals (any combination)'.
The role title includes 'Head of' suggesting people management, but the JD explicitly states this person will not manage large execution teams directly. Manages_people set to false.
